title: psephellus amblyolepis
intro: Psephellus amblyolepis, also known as the mountain dahlia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Psephellus amblyolepis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially treating various skin issues such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Psephellus amblyolepis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
